signals.idea: Build an LLM-powered custom effects editor. Users pick a category (entrance/exit/continuous), describe what they want in natural language, an LLM generates Remotion effect code, it gets validated via the existing Sucrase compile pipeline, previewed on a sample clip, and saved as a resource (type: 'effect') in the resources table (not the effects table). Users can also edit existing effects by describing changes. Effects are public or private and appear in the effect dropdowns alongside built-ins. Key pieces: (1) Migrate effect storage from effects table to resources table with metadata containing name, slug, code, category, description. (2) LLM edge function that receives a prompt + category + optional existing code and returns valid Remotion effect JSX. (3) Validation UI that compiles the returned code, shows errors if any, and lets user preview. (4) Effect creator/editor panel accessible from the effects tab in clip properties. (5) Resource hooks for querying user + public effects. (6) Wire into DynamicEffectRegistry so saved effects appear in dropdowns. (7) User selects category (entrance/exit/continuous) before prompting.

unresolved_flags.concern: Effect migration: Step 3 changes the registry source before any backfill or dual-read path, so existing custom effects can disappear during rollout.
Effect identity: Public effects keyed only by `custom:slug` will collide across users, causing the wrong code to register, render, or reopen for editing.
Effect validation: The current Sucrase compiler never throws on bad effect code, so the planned validation/error UI cannot reliably detect generation failures or gate saving.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 6.0. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 3.

items: User can select a category (entrance/exit/continuous), type a natural language description, and receive generated Remotion effect code from the LLM edge function
Generated code compiles successfully via the Sucrase pipeline and produces a valid React component
User can preview the generated effect on a sample clip before saving
User can edit an existing effect by describing desired changes in natural language
Saved effects appear in the appropriate category dropdown (entrance/exit/continuous) alongside built-in effects
Effects are stored in the resources table with type 'effect' and proper metadata (name, slug, code, category, description)
Public effects from other users appear in dropdowns; private effects only appear for their creator
Applying a custom effect from the dropdown renders correctly in the Remotion player and final export
Compilation errors from invalid LLM output are shown inline with a retry option
The edge function is rate-limited and authenticated, following the existing ai-prompt pattern

unresolved_flags.concern: Effect migration: Step 3 changes the registry source before any backfill or dual-read path, so existing custom effects can disappear during rollout.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The current editor bootstraps runtime effects only from `useEffects()` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.tsx:18-22`, and `useEffects()` reads only `public.effects` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffects.ts:13-17`. The plan switches `useEffectRegistry` to resources in Step 3, but migration/back-compat is deferred to Step 10, so any existing `custom:*` clips lose their code source unless the rollout dual-reads or backfills first.

unresolved_flags.concern: Effect identity: Public effects keyed only by `custom:slug` will collide across users, causing the wrong code to register, render, or reopen for editing.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Dynamic effects are stored in the registry by normalized name only in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts:13-20,51-52`, clip rendering also strips `custom:` and looks up by bare slug in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:93-106`, and the UI writes clip references as `custom:${slug}` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:174-183`. The existing table only guarantees `unique (user_id, slug)` in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/supabase/migrations/20260325090000_create_video_editor_tables.sql:42-54`, so once public effects from multiple users are merged, duplicate slugs become ambiguous.

unresolved_flags.concern: Effect validation: The current Sucrase compiler never throws on bad effect code, so the planned validation/error UI cannot reliably detect generation failures or gate saving.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `compileWithTransform()` catches all transform/runtime errors and returns a fallback React component instead of surfacing an error in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/effects/compileEffect.tsx:41-77`, and `compileEffectAsync()` just returns that component in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/effects/compileEffect.tsx:94-95`. The plan's Step 6 assumes client validation can 'show compilation errors' and disable save based on failure, but the current API contract does not expose failure as a detectable result.
